title: orobanche salviae subsp. echinopis
intro: Orobanche salviae subsp. echinopis is a parasitic plant species that belongs to the broomrape family (Orobanchaceae) and is commonly known as the echinop's broomrape or Orobanche echinopii. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Orobanche salviae subsp. echinopis include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, and is considered a valuable remedy in traditional folk medicine.
